---
title: "Project Structure"
description: "Recommended layout for a Rustimate project."
---

<br>
I propose a well-organised Rustimate project which separates scripts, assets, and outputs.
Feel free to have your own structure

## Layout

```
project/
│
├─ scripts/
│   ├─ intro.rsl
│   └─ binary_search.rsl
│
├─ assets/
│   ├─ images/
│   └─ icons/
│
├─ output/
│   ├─ intro.mp4
│   └─ binary_search.mp4
│
└─ rustimate.toml
```

## Folder purposes

| Folder / File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `scripts/` | All `.rsl` source files |
| `assets/` | Images, icons, and other static files used by scenes |
| `output/` | Rendered video and image exports |
| `rustimate.toml` | Project-level configuration (optional) |

## Docs project layout

If you are building a Quarto documentation site alongside Rustimate, the structure may extend to:

```
docs/
│
├─ _quarto.yml
├─ index.qmd
├─ installation.qmd
├─ cli.qmd
├─ rsl-reference.qmd
├─ quickstart.qmd
├─ examples.qmd
├─ rustimate-guide.qmd
├─ project-structure.qmd
├─ editor-integration.qmd
└─ troubleshooting.qmd
```
